ai_scope_discriminator: Confirm AI/ML payload from NAMES/MANIFEST only, never object bytes. lakeFS models data as Git-like repositories fronting an object store: (1) Repository IDs are the bucket-equivalent. Enumerate them with an authed `GET /api/v1/repositories` (or, when listable, the S3 gateway ListBuckets which renders each repo as a bucket in a ListAllMyBucketsResult/ListBucketResult XML body) and read the `id` field; ML intent shows in repo names like `models`, `datasets`, `training-data`, `checkpoints`, `mlflow`, `artifacts`, `features`, `feature-store`, `embeddings`, `dvc`, `ml-`, `-experiments`. (2) Within a repo, list keys (read-only `GET .../refs/{ref}/objects/ls` or S3 ListObjects) and classify by path/extension WITHOUT downloading: `*.safetensors`, `*.ckpt`, `*.pt`, `*.gguf`, `*.onnx`, `*.h5`, `*.pkl`, `*.parquet`, `MLmodel`, `conda.yaml`, `tokenizer.json`, `config.json` under a `model/` or `checkpoints/` prefix. The branch/commit graph (`main`, `experiment-*`, dated commits) over those prefixes confirms this is a versioned ML artifact store, not a generic file dump. Names plus the lakeFS commit metadata ARE the finding; object content stays unread.
fingerprint_markers: GET /api/v1/healthcheck -> HTTP 204 No Content, empty body, no auth
GET /api/v1/config -> JSON config object (storage_config, version) on many builds
Response header X-Lakefs-Server-ID present (anti-replay header added by default)
UI HTML/JS bundle references 'lakeFS', '/setup_lakefs', and the lakeFS logo/title; React SPA served at /
GET /api/v1/usage-report/summary returns aggregate metrics unauth on < 1.71.0 (CVE-2025-64179 marker)
S3 gateway mode: virtual-host bucket = lakeFS repository id; ListBucketResult/ListAllMyBucketsResult XML keyed on s3_gateway.domain_name
fp_guards: Require a lakeFS-unique JSON or header signal, NOT a bare ListBucketResult or open port 8000 (those match generic S3/MinIO/Ceph RGW). The discriminating probe is GET /api/v1/setup_lakefs returning {"state":...} or GET /api/v1/healthcheck returning 204 with the X-Lakefs-Server-ID header.

MinIO answers /minio/health/live and emits Server: MinIO and x-amz-* headers but has NO /api/v1/setup_lakefs and NO X-Lakefs-Server-ID; SeaweedFS/Ceph RGW lack /api/v1/setup_lakefs entirely. Negative-match these to strip generic object stores.
Do not infer AI/ML from port 8000 alone (8000 is a common dev-server / Django / FastAPI / SimpleHTTPServer port); demand the lakeFS API marker first.
state:"initialized" alone is not a finding (auth-on-default, repo listing requires creds). Only state:"not_initialized" indicates a claimable fresh install.
A reverse proxy / LBot-style catch-all can echo 'lakeFS' in title; verify via the structured /api/v1/setup_lakefs JSON enum value, not the HTML title string.

cves.desc: Missing authorization on GET /api/v1/usage-report/summary lets an unauthenticated attacker read aggregate API usage metrics (service activity/uptime leak). CVSS 5.3. Fixed in lakeFS 1.71.0.
lakeFS S3 gateway does not validate request timestamps on signed requests, allowing replay of a captured signed request until credentials rotate. CVSS 6.5. Fixed in lakeFS 1.75.0.

pivot_paths: GET /api/v1/setup_lakefs -> read state; not_initialized = fresh/claimable install where first-admin can be created without auth (do NOT POST /setup_lakefs; observe only)
GET /api/v1/config -> storage backend type + lakeFS version for CVE scoping (list-only)
GET /api/v1/usage-report/summary -> on < 1.71.0 returns metrics unauth (CVE-2025-64179), confirms version band without credentials
GET /api/v1/repositories -> repository id list (auth required; names only if creds in scope)
S3 gateway ListBuckets / ListObjects -> repo and key names as S3 XML (auth required; metadata only, never GetObject)
restraint_note: Names and manifest metadata are the finding. Read only: repository ids, branch/tag/commit names, and object KEY listings (objects/ls or S3 ListObjects XML). Never issue GetObject / download object bytes (that is exfiltration of AI artifacts/training data). Never POST /api/v1/setup_lakefs or otherwise claim the first-admin on a not_initialized install; record claimable state, do not exercise it. No branch create/commit/merge, no repository create/delete, no GC-rule or branch-protection change, no policy/credential writes. The usage-report and healthcheck reads are passive GETs only. Capture the version for CVE scoping; do not test CVE-2025-68671 by replaying captured signed requests.
